key_items: Bitcoin Dives Below $75K for First Time in a Month as Crypto Liquidations Near $1 Billion
Fed minutes turn Bitcoin's rate-cut trade into a hike-risk problem — majority of policymakers signal no rate cuts expected
Trump Media (DJT) Moves to Sell Bitcoin as Losses Reach $455 Million — transferring 2,650 BTC signals institutional weakness
reasoning: Near-term technicals and macro headwinds are creating downward pressure: BTC broke below $75K with $1B in liquidations, Fed minutes eliminated rate-cut expectations that fueled the rally, and high-profile sellers (Trump Media, Mark Cuban) are exiting positions. Positive structural factors (bond crisis supercycle theory, MoonPay integration, corporate accumulation) are being overwhelmed by immediate negative catalysts in the 1-7 day swing timeframe.
risks: Further Fed hawkishness or additional macro data pointing to higher rates could trigger a cascade to $72.5K-$70K support levels with accelerating liquidations.
